name: Primorsky Krai (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the movement of Primorye forward to new heights
honesty, loyalty and the sea, on which the life of the region depends
the gold of the Ussuri tiger

description: A field divided diagonally from upper fly to lower hoist by a narrow white stripe; the upper-hoist triangle is red and the lower-fly triangle is blue, with a golden walking Ussuri tiger placed in the upper-hoist red triangle.
history: Adopted on 22 February 1995 by the Primorsky Krai Duma and reaffirmed by oblast law in 2003.

features.symbolism: the diagonal arrangement carries the white stripe upward and forward, symbolising the krai's continuing progress; the red triangle at the hoist and the blue triangle at the fly together echo the colours of the Russian flag without repeating its layout.
a golden walking Ussuri (Amur) tiger placed in the upper hoist; the tiger is the principal heraldic charge of the krai's coat of arms and the iconic native predator of the region.
